label: Long-Term Investing Success
description: The letter emphasizes the power of long-term investments in businesses with favorable economics and trustworthy management, exemplified by Coca-Cola and American Express.
key_quote: Over time, it takes just a few winners to work wonders.

label: Capital Allocation Prudence
description: Buffett acknowledges that most of his capital allocation decisions have been only so-so, highlighting the importance of a few truly good decisions and luck.
key_quote: In 58 years of Berkshire management, most of my capital-allocation decisions have been no better than so-so.

label: Shareholder Trust and Values
description: The letter expresses gratitude for the enduring trust of Berkshire's shareholders, emphasizing their philanthropic nature and focus on long-term value creation.
key_quote: We are grateful for their enduring trust, a relationship that often spans much of their adult lifetime.

label: Business-Picking over Stock-Picking
description: Buffett clarifies that Berkshire focuses on investing in businesses with strong fundamentals rather than engaging in short-term stock trading.
key_quote: Charlie and I are not stock-pickers; we are business-pickers.

label: Market Inefficiency Recognition
description: The letter acknowledges that stock markets are not always efficient and that opportunities arise from mispricing, particularly for long-term investors.
key_quote: It’s crucial to understand that stocks often trade at truly foolish prices, both high and low.

label: Operating Earnings Success
description: Berkshire's operating earnings reached a record high in 2022, demonstrating the company's financial strength.
key_quote: Berkshire had a good year in 2022.
